methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M total raised proxy.

private.name: Seasats
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Bedrock Ocean
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Havoc AI is a Series A-stage frontier-tech company operating in Ocean & Maritime, founded by Paul Lwin and Joe Turner. The company has raised $200M to date. The relevant comparables universe consists of two private peers: Seasats and Bedrock Ocean. No public companies in this narrow sector were identified, limiting triangulation to private market precedent.

The valuation analysis applies stage-progression methodology, which is the applicable approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $200M as a proxy for implied current value, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $400M. This represents the median point in the triangulated range.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with $400M as the central estimate. The narrow methodology set and absence of public comparables represent material constraints; the single-method triangulation relies heavily on the stage-progression step-up assumption holding for this specific frontier sector. Key uncertainties include the applicability of standard venture step-up multiples to an emerging ocean-tech market, and the degree to which the two identified private peers are truly comparable on revenue, profitability, or capital efficiency metrics. Additional diligence on peer-level unit economics would strengthen conviction in this range.
